sections.paragraph:
προσδέω 1 aor. προσέδησα; pass. pf. 3 impv. pl. προσδεδέσθωσαν 1 Cl 27:1; ptc. fem. acc. προσδεδεμένην (Just., D. 53, 2 [on Mk 11:4]) to fasten by binding, tie, bind

ⓐ lit. (Hdt.; Hippocr.; Diod S 17, 41; Lucian, Dial. Deor. 6, 5; Ps.-Lucian, Asinus 38; Plut., Pericl. 167 [28, 2]; SIG 1169, 41; 4 Macc 9:26; TestSol 10:8 P; Just., D. 53, 2 [on Mk 11:4]) τινά MPol 14:1 to a stake.

ⓑ fig., pass. (Sir 18:32; Jos., Ant. 5, 135 ἡδονῇ προσδεδεμένοι) be bound securely τινί to someone 1 Cl 27:1.—DELG s.v. 1 δέω.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pf. = perfect

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. pl. = plural

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

ptc. ptc. = participle

fem. fem. = feminine

acc. acc. = accusative

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 877
